preconditions.text: If there is any question that a potential donor may be an excluded source, the CIO gift review official, in collaboration with OGC, should review recommendations regarding excluded sources made by the Advisory Committee to the Director in its May 2016 report.

steps.text: Contact OFR, Office of Debt Management for disposition of prohibited or unwanted gifts.
Ensure written notification accompanies all gift returns.
Use first-class mail to forward reimbursements to donors.
Contact the CDC Ethics and Compliance Activity for guidance regarding disposition of gifts to individual employees.
Return tangible items to the donor.
Pay the donor market value subject to OGC approval.
Destroy items, share within the office, or donate, subject to supervisor or organization head approval.

constraints.text: CDC will not accept a gift conditioned upon any expenditure that cannot be covered by the gift itself or from income earned from the gift.
Gifts may not be accepted if the conditions placed on the gift create a conflict of interest.
Gifts tendered in order to secure an implied or direct endorsement are not accepted.
Donors from commercial organizations must be advised that the acceptance of a gift does not constitute an implied or direct endorsement.
Gifts may not be accepted when the donor expresses an expectation of receiving a future benefit.

steps.text: Accept funds from an outside organization as a gift.
Deposit accepted funds into the appropriate CDC gift fund account.
Seek guidance from the CDC Ethics and Compliance Activity to avoid conflicts of interest.
Seek guidance from OGC to ensure conditions of grant award documents are legally appropriate.

preconditions.text: If an employee receives funds from an outside organization that supports the costs of CDC activities.

steps.text: Contact OGC regarding guidance on legal issues in the administration of gifts.
Negotiate with attorneys representing potential donors or donors’ estates.
Determine whether a potential donor is a prohibited source.
Coordinate with the Ethics Division, OGC, on the review of gifts as needed.
Review approval of the gift and all associated paperwork prior to requesting budget authority or CANs.
Contact ECA regarding gifts to individual employees that are not covered by this policy.
Reach out to ECA with questions regarding acceptance of gifts as an individual.
Forward the cash or cash equivalents for deposit to the Deputy Chief Financial Officer within 24 hours of receipt.
Include a brief memorandum stating that the gift is pre-decisional with the gift transmittal.
Mail gift fund checks to the specified USPS address.
Mail gift fund checks to the specified Private Carrier Delivery address.
Transmit wired funds via pay.gov.
Deposit the gift in a suspense account unless it is officially accepted within the 24-hour period.
Return monetary gift checks more than 90 days old to the recipient organization for return to the donor for reissuance.
Determine if the proposed gift has been recommended for approval by the Conflict-of-Interest panel.
Decide whether to accept the gift.
Notify the donor of the determination for both monetary and non-monetary gifts.

preconditions.text: For monetary gifts valued at not more than $2 million and unconditional gifts of personal property valued at not more than $5,000, authority rests with the CIO director.
For monetary gifts of more than $2 million, authority rests with the CDC COO.
The timeframe may be extended in cases where the decision on the acceptance of a gift is difficult.

steps.text: Prepare acceptance letter from CDC to the donor stating that the monetary gift is being deposited into the component’s gift fund account.
Deposit the monetary gift into the gift fund account if accepted.
Record the monetary gift as conditional or unconditional and code it with the appropriate program segment code.
Return uncashed checks to the donor if the monetary gift is not accepted or if the donor refuses to accept CDC terms.
Draw a refund check from the Department of the Treasury if the donor's check has already been deposited.
Thank the donor for non-monetary gifts and inform them that the donation will be considered for acceptance.
Notify the Logistics Management Branch, OSSAM, of proposals for personal property gifts.
Submit a copy of the document accepting the personal property gift to OSSAM.

preconditions.text: If the monetary gift is accepted, proceed to deposit the gift.
If the monetary gift is not accepted, return any uncashed checks.

steps.text: Create a basic record in the on-line property system upon notification of acceptance of the gift by the CIO property custodian.
Request in writing that the Chief Financial Officer invest the excess funds in interest-bearing obligations of the United States if excess funds are $50,000 or more.
Include information concerning when these investments will be redeemed in order to meet planned outlays in the notification.
Include the amount of funds invested, the source of funds, and any recommendations regarding purchase of the U.S. Treasury bills, notes, or bonds in the director's memorandum.
Return the monies upon liquidation of investments to the same gift fund account to be used for the benefit of the CIOs.

constraints.text: Invested gift funds and income from these investments retain the same limitations as the original gifts.
If an investment is redeemed before the maturity date, there may be loss of principal.
The spend plan must be aligned with the investment of the securities and redemption of the securities.

steps.text: Determine if there is sensitivity in any matter pending before CDC that would affect the interest of the donor.
Assess the effects of accepting the gift on entities that are inside/outside the agency or component.
Consult the Office of the General Counsel to provide guidance on problematic issues about the acceptance of the gift.
Evaluate the practical impact of the gift within CDC or CIO.
Clarify why the gift is offered to CDC or CIO.
